direct_input.stay_intent: 我想一家人安安静静地在一起,不要太热闹,但是要让小辈记得这次过生日。

family.name: Mr. Gu Jianping (顾建平)
family.notes: Retired civil engineer; mild hypertension, prefers low-sodium; quiet, defers to Mrs. Shen on all hospitality decisions; takes an early walk every morning.

family.name: Gu Wenqian (顾文倩)
family.notes: Daughter; Shanghai-based brand strategist; the family's logistical anchor and English-language interface with staff; gluten-sensitive.

family.name: Lin Hao (林昊)
family.notes: Son-in-law; works in finance; northern Chinese background — slightly outside the Jiangnan register, conscious of getting the in-law role right; lactose-intolerant.

family.name: Lin Yuyao (林雨瑶) — called Yaoyao (瑶瑶)
family.notes: Granddaughter, age 3; naps 13:00–15:00; first international trip; the emotional center of the entire stay — every arrangement should quietly accommodate her without making her the announced subject.

family.name: Gu Wenbo (顾文博)
family.notes: Son; based in Singapore for work, cannot attend this stay; sending a separate birthday gift to the suite — coordination already underway with concierge.

notable_moments: Asked the concierge for a tea master who could speak to Suzhou-style green tea preparation, not just generic Chinese tea service
Spent an unhurried afternoon at the courtyard rather than the Summer Palace excursion she had pre-booked; politely cancelled day-of
Wrote a short note in semi-cursive (行书) on the in-suite stationery thanking the housekeeper by name
service_choices: In-suite Longjing and Biluochun on arrival
Light congee breakfast served at 07:30 in-suite, not the buffet
Quiet table at Country Kitchen, away from the open-fire counter
declined_or_unused: Pre-booked Summer Palace private tour (cancelled day-of, no rebooking)
Champagne welcome amenity (untouched; replaced with osmanthus tea on request)
stays.memory_artifact_excerpt: Guest preferred to shape her own pace. Cancellations were never about the offering — about the energy of the day. Read the weather and her morning posture before suggesting anything outdoors.

stays.party_composition: Mrs. Shen + Mr. Gu + daughter Wenqian + son Wenbo
notable_moments: Requested a round table (not a long table) for the New Year's Eve dinner so the family could face each other; specifically asked that the 鱼 (fish) course be plated whole and served last, head pointing toward Mr. Gu as eldest male
Daughter Wenqian quietly arranged a small calligraphy set in the suite as a surprise; Mrs. Shen used it every morning of the stay
Declined the property's lion dance performance schedule for their floor — preferred to watch from a distance on the lawn
service_choices: Round-table family dining, indoor private room
Spring couplets (春联) and 福 character placed on suite door without being asked twice
Early-morning beach walks before 07:00, before other guests
declined_or_unused: In-suite lion dance visit (declined — 'too loud for the suite, we'll watch from outside')
Kids' club for Wenbo's then-25-year-old age bracket (not applicable, but offered)
stays.memory_artifact_excerpt: The family's center of gravity is Mrs. Shen, but she never asks for anything in her own name — always framed as 'for my husband' or 'for the children.' Anticipate; do not ask twice.

stays.occasion: Pre-Christmas couple's trip, gift shopping for daughter's wedding
notable_moments: Spent two unhurried afternoons at The Butterfly Room; ordered the same osmanthus-jasmine pairing both days and asked the name of the tea sommelier
Requested the harbour-view suite be set with curtains half-drawn on arrival — 'the view is too much all at once'
Made a quiet inquiry at the concierge about a Suzhou-embroidery atelier in Sheung Wan; staff arranged a private viewing
service_choices: Butterfly Room afternoon tea, corner seating both days
In-suite breakfast: congee, youtiao, soy milk — never the Western set
Private car for the embroidery atelier visit, no tour guide
declined_or_unused: Asaya spa hammam — only Mr. Gu used the gym; Mrs. Shen used the suite bath only
Christmas afternoon tea theme menu (asked for the standard menu instead — 'we are not celebrating that holiday')
stays.memory_artifact_excerpt: Reads light very carefully. Half-drawn curtains is not a preference — it is a way of entering a place. Lead with restraint; the depth comes later.

stays.occasion: Mid-Autumn Festival, first stay as expanded family (daughter newly married, son-in-law joining for the first time)
stays.party_composition: Mrs. Shen + Mr. Gu + Wenqian + Lin Hao
notable_moments: Concierge sent moon cakes from a small Wan Chai bakery (not the hotel's branded box) on Mrs. Shen's standing request — she gifted one set to son-in-law Lin Hao's parents in Qingdao via Rosewood's shipping
Mrs. Shen requested the Mid-Autumn dinner be served at 18:30 sharp so they could move to the rooftop for moon-viewing at 19:45 — timing matched the actual moonrise, which she had checked herself
Lin Hao was seated to Mr. Gu's left at dinner; Mrs. Shen quietly confirmed the seating chart with the maître d' beforehand
service_choices: Moon-viewing arrangement on Asaya terrace, tea service only (no alcohol)
Round private dining room for the family dinner
Pomelo and traditional mooncakes — not the chocolate or ice-cream variants
declined_or_unused: Hotel-branded Mid-Autumn gift box (declined — sourced her own from Wan Chai)
Champagne pairing with dinner (replaced with aged pu-erh)
Rooftop bar music programming (asked for it to be lowered during their moon-viewing window)
stays.memory_artifact_excerpt: She is teaching the new son-in-law the family's rhythm without ever saying so. Seating, timing, and the choice of bakery are all instructions. Notice them; do not name them.

stays.occasion: Lunar New Year, daughter pregnant (second trimester)
stays.party_composition: Mrs. Shen + Mr. Gu + Wenqian (pregnant) + Lin Hao
notable_moments: Asked for the villa with the shortest, flattest walk from the buggy drop-off to the entrance — pregnancy not stated, inferred from the request
Pre-ordered a thermos of ginger-and-red-date tea (姜枣茶) to be in the villa each morning before the daughter woke
Politely asked staff to never offer raw fish, sashimi, or unpasteurized cheese to the daughter — without explanation; staff inferred and complied across all outlets
service_choices: Cantonese-leaning menu at Ta Khai instead of the seafood-forward set
Late-morning rather than dawn beach time — adjusted for daughter's nausea window
declined_or_unused: Spa pregnancy massage menu (Mrs. Shen declined on her daughter's behalf — 'not yet')
Long-tail boat excursion to nearby islands
stays.memory_artifact_excerpt: Mrs. Shen protects without announcing. The ginger tea, the menu adjustments, the villa choice — none were ever explained, all were correct. The family operates on inference; staff who can match that earn trust quickly.

stays.occasion: Qingming festival short stay; first trip with grandchild (then 12 months)
stays.party_composition: Mrs. Shen + Mr. Gu + Wenqian + Lin Hao + Yaoyao (infant)
notable_moments: Asked Madera-style restaurant Xi Yan to plate the grandchild's congee in a small ceramic bowl — 'not the plastic one, please' — and to serve it before the adult courses
Mrs. Shen never once requested a high chair to be placed at the table — she held Yaoyao through most meals; staff quietly kept a high chair within reach but unobtrusive
Requested the in-suite floral arrangement be removed on day 2 — 'the lilies are a bit strong for the baby'
service_choices: Connecting rooms, not a single large suite — privacy for the young family at night
Quiet corner table at every meal, always near a wall not a window (less distraction for the baby)
Stroller storage and discreet daily cleaning without being asked
declined_or_unused: Kids' welcome amenity (stuffed bear, branded — politely set aside)
In-room dining children's menu (Mrs. Shen prepared the baby's food herself from ingredients she brought)
Photographer for the baby's first hotel stay (declined — 'we have our own photos')
stays.memory_artifact_excerpt: The grandchild is the center of every arrangement but is never the announced subject. Mrs. Shen's care is exercised through the room, the table, the flowers — not through requests about the child directly. Read the environment, not the child.

observed_register: family ritual (round table, seating order, whole-fish symbolism)
quiet (curtains half-drawn, music lowered, corner tables)
observed_avoidances: Western holiday theming (Christmas, Western New Year)
branded gift boxes and corporate amenities
raw or unpasteurized foods for the family
high-volume entertainment (lion dance in-suite, rooftop music during family moments)
champagne and alcohol pairings (replaced with tea)
being addressed as the subject of celebration — prefers attention on husband, daughter, grandchild
tourist-facing excursions (Peak Tram, Summer Palace tour, island-hopping)

recurring_staff_relationships.note: Has handled three of her Hong Kong-area stays; trusted to source off-property items (Wan Chai bakery moon cakes, Sheung Wan embroidery atelier) without prompting. Mrs. Shen addresses him as 陈先生 and remembers his children's names.

recurring_staff_relationships.staff: Ms. Wong, Butterfly Room tea sommelier
recurring_staff_relationships.note: Mrs. Shen specifically asked for her by name during the 2022 stay; the osmanthus-jasmine pairing is her recommendation and has become Mrs. Shen's standing tea on property.

Anticipated the daughter's pregnancy without being told — arranged the ginger-and-red-date tea and the flat-walk villa. Mrs. Shen sent a hand-written thank-you card from Suzhou after the stay, which is unusual for her.

derived_from_history.cross_property_pattern: Across six years and four properties, Mrs. Shen's stays show a slow widening of the circle — from a quiet couple's trip, to family gatherings around festivals, to the first trip with the grandchild. The constants are: she chooses the rhythm, never announces it; she protects the youngest and the eldest at the table without naming the protection; and she rewards staff who infer rather than ask. Her trust is earned by what is *not* offered: branded amenities, loud experiences, and direct attention to her as celebrant.

cultural_profile_hint.cultural_lens: East Asian Jiangnan classical · Wu literati matriarch
register: seasonal and festival timing (real moonrise, solar terms)
Suzhou garden aesthetics (restraint, framed views, half-revealed)
family ritual (seating order, whole-course symbolism, round tables)
calligraphy and embroidery as quiet markers
indirection — care expressed through environment, not language
cultural_profile_hint.current_phase: Earth — the matriarchal grounding phase; centered on family continuity, the transmission of rhythm to the next generation, and the quiet authorship of the gathering rather than its celebration of self
emotional_anchors: the grandchild's first international memory — to be felt, not photographed
the husband's milestone-eve composure (his own 65th approaches next year)
the daughter as the bridge generation, learning to host her own family
the son-in-law's inclusion — making him belong without making him perform
her own 60th as a hinge, not a peak — a turning toward the role of elder
avoid: addressing her as the celebrant; route felicitations through husband or daughter
branded or themed birthday production (cake parade, staff song, signage)
Western luxury markers (champagne towers, rose petal turn-downs)
loud or high-stimulation experiences anywhere near nap time (13:00–15:00)
strong florals in family spaces — favor osmanthus, jasmine, or none
asking the family to articulate preferences in advance — read and offer instead
naming the grandchild as the subject of any arrangement, even when she is
tourist itineraries; the city is backdrop, the suite and the table are the stage
